content.text:
[Tue 2026-04-28 09:34 UTC] You are an information intelligence assistant. Prepare a concise industry news credibility brief.

## Goal
Summarize only trusted industry news from the last 24 hours. A story is trusted only when both conditions hold:
1. Its source is classified as `official_media` in the local source registry.
2. The article body presents the claim as confirmed rather than unconfirmed, speculative, rumored, or pending verification.

Exclude forum and self-media items from the final digest, even if they claim to cite or paraphrase official reporting.

Produce a concise final brief of trusted news in outputs/digest.md.

## Tool
- `python3 tools/list_sources.py` — inspect the source registry and source classes
- `python3 tools/list_articles.py` — inspect article ids, source classes, and priority order
- `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` — read the full text of an article before deciding trust

## OpenClaw execution (benchmark)
- Do not change OpenClaw `tool.exec` settings: keep **security** at **full** and **ask** at **off**.
- Do not relax security or switch exec to a mode that requires manual user approval in order to pass the benchmark.
- Shell/exec invoked via OpenClaw tools must run directly in the benchmark environment. **No `/approve` paste or approval step—execute directly.**

## Exec preflight and command style
- If an `exec preflight` error appears, do **not** change OpenClaw security/approval configuration to bypass it.
- Keep OpenClaw `tool.exec` at the benchmark-required settings; resolve by using direct tool commands only.
- Execute commands as separate single-command invocations, one at a time.
- Do **not** chain commands with `&&` or `;`.
- Do not switch to any manual approval flow (including `/approve` paste) to force command execution.
I'll work through this systematically — inspect sources, identify official_media, read relevant articles, and produce the digest.

content.text: Good. Four official_media articles (art_001–004) and two to exclude (art_005 forum, art_006 self_media). Now I'll read each official_media article to check whether claims are confirmed or speculative.

id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

content.text:
id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

content.text:
id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

content.text:
id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

content.text:
All four articles read. Now I have a clear picture:

- **art_001** ✅ Confirmed (official launch, GA)
- **art_002** ✅ Confirmed (standard published, binding)
- **art_003** ✅ Confirmed (approved, funded, proceeding)
- **art_004** ❌ Explicitly **unconfirmed** — Aster Labs itself says the spinout claim is speculative; exclude from trusted digest
- **art_005** ❌ Forum source — excluded
- **art_006** ❌ Self-media source — excluded

arguments.content:
# Industry News Credibility Brief
**Generated:** 2026-04-28 09:34 UTC  
**Coverage window:** Last 24 hours  
**Trust criteria:** Source class = `official_media` AND claim presented as confirmed (not speculative/rumored/pending)

---

## ✅ Trusted Stories (3)

### 1. Vertex Cloud Launches Enterprise Inference Controls
- **Source:** Vertex Cloud Newsroom (`official_media`) — 4 hours ago
- **Status:** Confirmed — general availability announced, features shipping in production now
- **Summary:** Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments. The newsroom post confirms immediate general availability and lists customer-facing governance features already in production.

---

### 2. National Digital Authority Publishes AI Procurement Standard
- **Source:** National Digital Authority (`official_media`) — 8 hours ago
- **Status:** Confirmed — standard adopted, binding requirements in effect this quarter
- **Summary:** The National Digital Authority published its final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The standard is adopted immediately and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements taking effect this quarter.

---

### 3. NovaChip Confirms Packaging Line Expansion in Suzhou
- **Source:** NovaChip Investor Relations (`official_media`) — 12 hours ago
- **Status:** Confirmed — expansion approved, funded, and proceeding per published capacity plan
- **Summary:** NovaChip confirmed via an investor relations filing that a packaging line expansion in Suzhou has been approved and funded, with work proceeding this year as part of the company's published capacity plan.

---

## ❌ Excluded Items (3)

| ID | Title | Reason for Exclusion |
|----|-------|----------------------|
| art_004 | Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed | `official_media` source, but claim is **explicitly unconfirmed/speculative** per Aster Labs itself |
| art_005 | Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week | Source class = `forum` |
| art_006 | CreatorWire says Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by sources | Source class = `self_media` |

---

*Note: art_006 (CreatorWire) claims to confirm a Vertex Cloud supplier freeze, but as a `self_media` outlet it does not meet the source trust threshold regardless of its framing. It is excluded.*

content.text:
Done. Here's the summary of what was produced:

---

## Credibility Brief — 2026-04-28

**3 trusted stories** made the cut out of 6 total articles:

1. **Vertex Cloud** — Enterprise inference controls now GA (confirmed launch, features in production)
2. **National Digital Authority** — AI procurement standard published and binding this quarter
3. **NovaChip** — Suzhou packaging line expansion approved, funded, and underway

**3 items excluded:**
- **Aster Labs robotics spinout** (art_004) — passes the source test (official_media) but *fails* the confirmation test: Aster Labs' own press desk explicitly called the reports "unconfirmed and speculative"
- **DeviceTalk Forum layoffs thread** (art_005) — forum source, excluded outright
- **CreatorWire supplier freeze** (art_006) — self-media source, excluded regardless of its "confirmed by sources" framing

Full digest written to **`outputs/digest.md`**.
